tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Thunder Cricket is a reaction bait — its blade vibration and flash trigger instinctual strikes even when bass are lethargic in cold water. Use a lift-and-drop retrieve with brief pauses. Most hits come on the drop.'
KVD: 'When wind pushes into shallow flats, bass edge up to feed. Fish the edges of grass, rocks, or flats where bait accumulates. Fish shallow first, then work deeper on windy days.'

newOpinions.defaultTip: KVD: 'Keep contact with the bottom — reaction bites happen low in the water column. Change cadence often. Adjust rod angle to feel blade vibration without snagging.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Cold water Thunder Cricket: cast toward structure or transitions, let sink just above cover/grass, lift-and-drop retrieve with brief pauses. The key isn't speed — it's triggering instinctive reaction bites. Slow enough for blade to stay in strike zone but fast enough for vibration.'

KVD: 'Medium-heavy casting rod with backbone, 6.4-7.1:1 reel, fluorocarbon leader. Match blade color to water — gold in stained, chrome in clear. Keeping hookup ratio high is essential when fish are only following.'

KVD: 'Cold water bass bites get tougher below 55°F. Morning — wind blows into shallow flats, bass edge up. Afternoon — fish move to outside bends and deeper transition breaks. Target these transition zones for reaction strikes.'
